proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.code: chi-square: treatment_enzalutamide vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: treatment_enzalutamide+: rr=0.3614 (n=20076); -: rr=0.1589 (n=29924); diff=+0.2024.

analyses.code: chi-square: treatment_abiraterone vs objective_response
chi-square: treatment_docetaxel vs objective_response
chi-square: treatment_olaparib vs objective_response
chi-square: treatment_lu177_psma vs objective_response
chi-square: treatment_pembrolizumab vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: treatment_pembrolizumab+: rr=0.2387 (n=2384); -: rr=0.2403 (n=47616); diff=-0.0016.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant) have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with mcrpc=0.
Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
Patients with msi_high=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with msi_high=0.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with visceral_mets=0.
Patients with psma_high=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with psma_high=0.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher gleason_score is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a higher probability of objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher nlr is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher age_years is associated with a different probability of objective_response.

analyses.code: chi-square: mcrpc vs objective_response
chi-square: brca2_mutation vs objective_response
chi-square: ar_v7_positive vs objective_response
chi-square: msi_high vs objective_response
chi-square: visceral_mets vs objective_response
chi-square: psma_high vs objective_response
Welch t-test: ecog_ps in responders vs non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean ecog_ps: responders 0.699, non-responders 0.828, diff=-0.128 (lower in responders).

analyses.code: Welch t-test: psa_ng_ml in responders vs non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean PSA: responders 28.24, non-responders 47.23, diff=-18.99 (lower in responders).

analyses.code: Welch t-test: gleason_score in responders vs non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean Gleason: responders 7.645, non-responders 7.640, diff=+0.005.

analyses.code: Welch t-test: albumin_g_dl in responders vs non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean albumin: responders 3.820, non-responders 3.796, diff=+0.024.

analyses.code: Welch t-test: weight_loss_pct_6mo in responders vs non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean weight_loss: responders 3.50, non-responders 3.95, diff=-0.45.

analyses.code: Welch t-test: crp_mg_l in responders vs non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean CRP: responders 5.69, non-responders 6.23, diff=-0.53.

analyses.code: Welch t-test: ldh_u_l in responders vs non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean LDH: responders 223.76, non-responders 224.11, diff=-0.35.

analyses.code: Welch t-test: nlr in responders vs non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean NLR: responders 3.486, non-responders 3.497, diff=-0.011.

analyses.code: Welch t-test: age_years in responders vs non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean age: responders 65.01, non-responders 64.98, diff=+0.024.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib increases objective_response specifically in patients with brca2_mutation=1 (predictive interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response specifically in patients with msi_high=1 (predictive interaction).
treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response specifically in patients with psma_high=1 (predictive interaction).
treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response more strongly in patients with ar_v7_positive=0 than in ar_v7_positive=1 (negative predictive interaction).
treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response more strongly in patients with mcrpc=0 (hormone-sensitive) than in mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant).

analyses.code: logit objective_response ~ treatment_olaparib * brca2_mutation; stratified two-proportion z-tests
analyses.result_summary: Stratified rr_olaparib - rr_off in BRCA2+: -0.0321 (p=0.050); in BRCA2-: +0.0031 (p=0.652). Logit interaction OR=0.749, p=0.045 — opposite direction from hypothesis: olaparib is associated with WORSE response in BRCA2+ patients, not better.

analyses.code: logit objective_response ~ treatment_pembrolizumab * msi_high; stratified two-proportion z-tests
analyses.result_summary: In MSI-H: rr_pembro=0.1772 (n=79) vs rr_off=0.1760 (n=1449); diff=+0.0012 (p=0.978). Interaction OR=1.017, p=0.956. No predictive effect found.

analyses.code: logit objective_response ~ treatment_lu177_psma * psma_high; stratified two-proportion z-tests
analyses.result_summary: In PSMA-high: rr_lu177=0.2376 (n=4486) vs rr_off=0.2388 (n=25476); diff=-0.0011 (p=0.868). Interaction OR=0.947, p=0.363. No predictive effect.

analyses.code: logit objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide * ar_v7_positive
analyses.result_summary: In ar_v7=0: enza diff=+0.2510 (n_enza=16057, p~0); in ar_v7=1: enza diff=+0.0085 (n_enza=4019, p=0.257). Interaction OR=0.290, p=1.4e-93. Strong predictive interaction confirming hypothesis.

analyses.code: logit objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide * mcrpc
analyses.result_summary: In mcrpc=0: enza diff=+0.4403 (n_enza=9039, p~0); in mcrpc=1: enza diff=+0.0077 (n_enza=11037, p=0.084). Interaction OR=0.138, p~0. Strong predictive interaction confirming hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response only in the joint subgroup mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0; in any other (mcrpc, ar_v7) combination there is no clinically meaningful effect.
Among BRCA2-mutated patients, no available treatment increases objective_response above baseline.

analyses.code: stratify by (mcrpc, ar_v7_positive) crosstab; two-proportion z-tests on enza vs no-enza
analyses.result_summary: (mcrpc=0, arv7=0): enza diff=+0.5459 (rr 0.7167 vs 0.1708, n_enza=7246, p~0). (mcrpc=0, arv7=1): diff=+0.0136 (p=0.234). (mcrpc=1, arv7=0): diff=+0.0084 (p=0.089). (mcrpc=1, arv7=1): diff=+0.0045 (p=0.648). Triple-interaction logistic confirms enza:mcrpc=-2.44 (p~0), enza:arv7=-2.41 (p<1e-26), enza:mcrpc:arv7=+2.38 (p<1e-26).

analyses.code: two-proportion z-tests for each treatment within brca2_mutation=1
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA2+: olaparib diff=-0.0321 (p=0.050); enzalutamide diff=+0.0063 (p=0.539); abiraterone diff=+0.0209 (p=0.057); docetaxel diff=-0.0084 (p=0.443); lu177_psma diff=+0.0193 (p=0.177); pembrolizumab diff=+0.0115 (p=0.620). No treatment improves response in BRCA2+ at p<0.05; if anything, olaparib is borderline harmful here.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the full feature set, the largest treatment-effect modifiers of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response are mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high, and psa_ng_ml (each suppressing benefit).
For treatments other than enzalutamide, no feature × treatment logistic interaction reaches strong statistical significance after the screen.

analyses.code: for each (treatment, feature): logit objective_response ~ treatment * feature; report interaction p
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions all involve enzalutamide: enza:mcrpc (p~0), enza:ar_v7_positive (p=1.4e-93), enza:psa_ng_ml (p=1.2e-72, slope=-0.0087/ng/mL, weaker benefit at higher PSA), enza:brca2_mutation (p=3.3e-42), enza:msi_high (p=1.8e-19), enza:ecog_ps (p=1.6e-5).

analyses.code: examine all non-enzalutamide rows of the interaction screen
analyses.result_summary: The strongest non-enzalutamide interactions: lu177_psma:visceral_mets (p=0.010), abiraterone:brca2_mutation (p=0.031), pembrolizumab:gleason_score (p=0.033), olaparib:brca2_mutation (p=0.045). All are nominal-level and would not survive multiplicity correction across ~120 tests.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the (mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0) base subgroup, brca2_mutation=1 abolishes the treatment_enzalutamide benefit on objective_response.
Within the (mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0) base subgroup, msi_high=1 abolishes the treatment_enzalutamide benefit on objective_response.
Within the (mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0) base subgroup, psma_high does NOT modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect.
Within the (mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0) base subgroup, visceral_mets does NOT modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect.
Within the (mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0) base subgroup, continuous covariates (psa_ng_ml, ecog_ps, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l) do NOT modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect on the logit scale.

analyses.code: within base subgroup, two-proportion z-test by brca2_mutation
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2-: enza diff=+0.6067 (rr 0.7800 vs 0.1733, p~0); BRCA2+: enza diff=+0.0039 (p=0.821). brca2_mutation=1 effectively abolishes benefit.

analyses.code: within base subgroup, two-proportion z-test by msi_high
analyses.result_summary: msi_high=0: enza diff=+0.5630 (rr 0.7326 vs 0.1697, p~0); msi_high=1: enza diff=-0.0224 (p=0.524). msi_high=1 effectively abolishes benefit.

analyses.code: within base subgroup, two-proportion z-test by psma_high
analyses.result_summary: psma_high=0: enza diff=+0.5684; psma_high=1: enza diff=+0.5309. Difference of differences = -0.038 (no abolition).

analyses.code: within base subgroup, two-proportion z-test by visceral_mets
analyses.result_summary: visceral_mets=0: enza diff=+0.5486; visceral_mets=1: enza diff=+0.5348. Difference of differences = -0.014 (no abolition).

analyses.code: within base subgroup, logit obj_response ~ enza*x for each continuous covariate
analyses.result_summary: enza:psa_ng_ml p=0.726; enza:ecog_ps p=0.407; enza:albumin_g_dl p=0.482; enza:ldh_u_l p=0.199; enza:crp_mg_l p=0.552. None significant. PSA quartile-stratified analysis: diff ranges +0.530 to +0.556 across quartiles — no modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response by a large absolute margin only in the joint subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this subgroup the effect is essentially zero.
Within mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant disease), no biomarker subgroup shows a clinically meaningful treatment_enzalutamide benefit on objective_response.

analyses.code: two-proportion z-test on responder vs complement
analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup (n=15681, 31.4%): enza diff=+0.6257 (rr 0.7978 vs 0.1721, p~0). Complement (n=34319): enza diff=+0.0077 (rr 0.1606 vs 0.1530, p=0.054). 80x larger effect in subgroup.

analyses.code: stratified two-proportion z-tests on enza within mcrpc=1 by each biomarker
analyses.result_summary: Within mcrpc=1: arv7=0 diff=+0.0084 (p=0.089); arv7=1 diff=+0.0045 (p=0.648); brca2=0 diff=+0.0061 (p=0.190); brca2=1 diff=+0.0216 (p=0.118); msi=0 diff=+0.0088 (p=0.050); msi=1 diff=-0.0305 (p=0.239); psma=0 diff=-0.0065; psma=1 diff=+0.0172 (p=0.0026); visceral=0 diff=+0.0060; visceral=1 diff=+0.0143. All effects below ~0.02 absolute — no clinically meaningful subgroup in mCRPC.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for all features, the only treatment with a significant main effect on objective_response is treatment_enzalutamide; treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, and treatment_pembrolizumab have OR≈1.
For each non-enzalutamide treatment, no single binary biomarker stratum shows a treatment effect on objective_response of magnitude >0.05 absolute and p<0.01.

analyses.code: logit objective_response ~ all treatments + all features (no interactions)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ORs (p): enza 3.32 (~0); abiraterone 0.98 (0.32); docetaxel 1.00 (0.94); olaparib 1.03 (0.50); lu177_psma 1.03 (0.35); pembrolizumab 1.01 (0.79). Strongest negative-prognostic features: mcrpc OR=0.32 (~0); arv7 OR=0.50 (p=8e-109); brca2 OR=0.48 (p=6e-64); msi OR=0.65 (p=3e-9); ecog_ps OR=0.72; psa lower OR; weight loss negative; albumin positive.

analyses.code: for each non-enza treatment, stratify by each binary biomarker; report differences with |diff|>0.01 or p<0.10
analyses.result_summary: abiraterone × brca2_mutation=1 diff=+0.0209 (p=0.057, NS); olaparib × brca2_mutation=1 diff=-0.0321 (p=0.050, borderline negative); lu177_psma × visceral_mets=1 diff=-0.0243 (p=0.040, nominal). All other strata show |diff|<0.04. None reaches |diff|>0.05 with p<0.01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For treatments other than enzalutamide, no 2-binary-feature subgroup shows a treatment effect on objective_response with |diff|>0.02 and p<0.01 (after the screen).

analyses.code: for each non-enza treatment and each pair of binary features (mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high), enumerate 2-feature strata with n_on>=50 and n_off>=50; flag |diff|>0.02 and p<0.01
analyses.result_summary: No subgroup meeting the |diff|>0.02 and p<0.01 threshold was found for any of abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, or pembrolizumab. Relaxing to p<0.05 yielded only weak signals (largest |diff|≈0.043 for olaparib in BRCA2+/arv7-, p=0.019). No coherent positive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib decreases objective_response in patients with brca2_mutation=1 (an adjusted predictive interaction).
treatment_lu177_psma decreases objective_response in patients with visceral_mets=1.

analyses.code: within brca2_mutation=1, logit obj_response ~ olaparib + (full covariate set)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted within BRCA2+: olaparib coef=-0.266 (OR=0.766, p=0.058). Direction is negative but borderline; the unadjusted stratified diff was -0.0321 (p=0.050). Suggestive of NO benefit (and possibly harm) of olaparib within BRCA2-mutated patients in this cohort.

analyses.code: within visceral_mets=1, two-proportion z-test on lu177_psma
analyses.result_summary: visceral_mets=1: rr_lu177=0.2190 (n=1539) vs rr_off=0.2433 (n=8434); diff=-0.0243 (p=0.040). Magnitude small; nominal-only and would not survive screening multiplicity. Not clinically convincing.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 16 cells defined by all combinations of (mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high), only the (0,0,0,0) cell shows a treatment_enzalutamide vs no-enzalutamide objective_response difference greater than +0.10 absolute.

treatment_enzalutamide × responder_subgroup interaction (where responder_subgroup = mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0) is highly significant after adjusting for age, ecog_ps, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, psma_high, albumin, ldh, weight_loss, crp, and nlr.

analyses.code: stratified two-proportion z-test of enza in each of 16 (mcrpc, arv7, brca2, msi) cells
analyses.result_summary: (0,0,0,0): diff=+0.6257 (n_enza=6325, p~0). All 13 other cells with usable size: |diff|<=0.13 and 12 of 13 have p>=0.075. Only the (0,0,0,0) cell shows the dramatic effect; all others show essentially no clinically meaningful enzalutamide benefit.

analyses.code: logit obj_response ~ enza*responder_subgroup + age + ecog_ps + visceral_mets + psa + psma + albumin + ldh + weight_loss + crp + nlr
analyses.result_summary: Interaction term enza:responder_subgroup coef=+2.94 (OR=18.98, p~0). Residual main-effect of enza outside subgroup OR=1.06 (p=0.048). Strong evidence for predictive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment assignment for treatment_enzalutamide is balanced with respect to baseline covariates (age, mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high, psma_high, ecog_ps, psa_ng_ml, albumin), implying that the observed enzalutamide subgroup effect is unlikely to be driven by confounding by indication.

FINAL: The treatment_enzalutamide -> objective_response causal effect is concentrated almost entirely in the subgroup mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; in this subgroup it raises the response rate from ~0.17 to ~0.80 (absolute diff ~+0.63). No other treatment in the dataset (treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, treatment_pembrolizumab) shows a positive treatment-effect subgroup of comparable size or magnitude.

analyses.code: compare mean of each baseline covariate between enza+ and enza-
analyses.result_summary: Enza+ vs Enza- means: age 64.91 vs 65.04 (diff -0.13); mcrpc 0.550 vs 0.550 (0.000); arv7 0.200 vs 0.201 (-0.001); brca2 0.101 vs 0.099 (+0.002); msi_high 0.029 vs 0.032 (-0.003); psma_high 0.599 vs 0.600 (-0.001); ecog_ps 0.795 vs 0.798 (-0.003); psa 42.49 vs 42.79 (-0.30); albumin 3.801 vs 3.803 (-0.002). Excellent balance — assignment looks essentially random with respect to measured covariates.

analyses.code: summary of all preceding analyses
analyses.result_summary: The (mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0, brca2_mutation=0, msi_high=0) subgroup contains 15681 patients (31.4% of cohort) and has response rate 0.42 overall vs 0.16 outside this subgroup. Within it, enzalutamide drives response 0.798 vs 0.172, +0.626 absolute, OR ~19 (p~0). Outside it, enza diff=+0.008. Adjusted enza:subgroup interaction term coef=+2.94, p~0. No comparable positive subgroup found for any other agent; olaparib in BRCA2+ is borderline negative.
